METADATA.description: Comprehensive Utah fishing and boating lakes database with fish species, weather stations, wind patterns, and physical characteristics

NORTHERN.label: Northern Utah
NORTHERN.description: Cache, Box Elder, Weber, Davis, Morgan counties — Great Salt Lake basin and Bear River drainage

WASATCH.label: Central / Wasatch Front & Back
WASATCH.description: Salt Lake, Utah, Wasatch, Summit counties — Wasatch Range, Heber Valley, Park City corridor

NORTHEASTERN.label: Northeastern Utah (Uinta Basin)
NORTHEASTERN.description: Duchesne, Uintah, Daggett counties — Uinta Mountains, Green River drainage

SOUTHEASTERN.label: Southeastern Utah (Castle Country)
SOUTHEASTERN.description: Carbon, Emery, Sanpete, Grand counties — Wasatch Plateau, Price River drainage
SOUTHEASTERN.nwsOffice: SLC / GJT (Grand Junction WFO for some areas)

SOUTHERN.label: Southern Utah
SOUTHERN.description: Sevier, Piute, Garfield, Wayne, Iron, Washington, Kane counties — High Plateaus, Dixie, Colorado Plateau
SOUTHERN.nwsOffice: SLC / VEF (Las Vegas WFO for Washington County)

BEAR_LAKE.label: Bear Lake Region
BEAR_LAKE.description: Rich County — Bear Lake straddles UT/ID border

fishSpecies.notes: Common carp ~90% of biomass. White bass and walleye are top targets. June sucker is federally threatened — must be released.

bestSeasons.iceFishing: December–February (when safe ice forms — rare due to shallow depth)

windPatterns.dominant: NW afternoon thermal/frontal, SE canyon drainage from Spanish Fork
windPatterns.hazards: Extremely shallow — 15 mph wind creates 3 ft waves, 40 mph creates 10-12 ft waves. Short-period chop very dangerous. Water can shift several feet between north and south ends.
windPatterns.thermalPattern: SE thermal builds 10 AM–1 PM from Spanish Fork Canyon. NW prefrontal/north flow can arrive any time.

nearbyStations.notes: KPVU (Provo Airport) is closest ASOS. FPS (Flight Park South) captures thermal onset. QSF (Spanish Fork) is 2-hour early indicator for SE thermal.

fishSpecies.notes: Tiger muskie and wiper make this a diverse multi-species fishery. Smallmouth bass are strong summer targets.

windPatterns.dominant: Canyon-channeled winds from Provo River corridor, afternoon thermals from Heber Valley
windPatterns.hazards: Can develop significant afternoon chop, especially upper reservoir near dam

nearbyStations.notes: KHCR (Heber Valley Airport) is closest ASOS. Shares wind corridor with Deer Creek — Arrowhead (SND) relevant for upper-level indicators.

fishSpecies.notes: Blue Ribbon fishery. Excellent multi-species warmwater and trout. Walleye and smallmouth bass are trophy targets.

windPatterns.dominant: South canyon thermal drawn up Provo Canyon (venturi effect). Arrowhead Summit (SND) triggers arrival.
windPatterns.hazards: Canyon funneling can produce sudden strong winds 12-18 mph. South wind is the key thermal; N wind means cold front.
windPatterns.thermalPattern: South thermal builds 1–4 PM when Heber Valley is cooler than Utah Valley. SND at 12+ mph = wind at dam in ~60 min.
windPatterns.bestCalm: Early morning before 10 AM, after 6 PM

nearbyStations.notes: DCC (Deer Creek Dam) is ground truth. SND (Arrowhead/Sundance) is the key high-elevation trigger at 8,252 ft. UTPCY (Provo Canyon MP10) shows canyon mouth draw.

willard-bay.name: Willard Bay Reservoir
willard-bay.state_park: Willard Bay State Park (North & South marinas)

fishSpecies.notes: Premier warmwater fishery. Wiper (white bass x striped bass hybrid) are the signature species — fights hard, schools aggressively.

windPatterns.dominant: North gap wind from Wasatch Front. South thermal 170-220° in summer afternoons.
windPatterns.hazards: Shallow like Utah Lake — chop builds fast. Frontal passages bring strong NW-N gusts.
windPatterns.bestCalm: Early morning, after frontal passage settles

nearbyStations.notes: UR328 (Willard) is closest. KOGD and KBMC bracket north/south. KHIF (Hill AFB) is reliable military station. BLPU1 (Ben Lomond) is high-elevation ridge indicator.

bear-lake.name: Bear Lake
bear-lake.state_park: Bear Lake State Park (multiple marinas: Cisco Beach, Rendezvous Beach, North Beach, Eastside)

fishSpecies.notes: 4 endemic species found nowhere else on Earth. Bear Lake cutthroat trout are a unique strain. January cisco dipnetting season is a beloved tradition.

bestSeasons.cisco: January (dipnetting during spawning run — special season)

windPatterns.dominant: Strong westerly winds year-round, especially winter/spring. Located in belt of strongest westerlies in northern Rockies.
windPatterns.hazards: HIGH WIND HAZARD. Afternoon thermals build rapidly. Sudden high winds can rise without warning — Bear Lake has claimed boats. Large fetch (20+ miles) creates dangerous swells.
windPatterns.thermalPattern: Afternoon thermal winds from the west, strongest June–August. Morning calm typically holds until 10-11 AM.
windPatterns.bestCalm: Early morning before 10 AM. Overcast/rainy days suppress thermals.

nearbyStations.notes: KLGU (Logan-Cache Airport) is nearest ASOS but 45+ miles away. BERU1 (Bear River) is closest automated station. Limited real-time lake-level wind data — significant coverage gap. Consider WeatherFlow station at Cisco Beach.

fishSpecies.notes: Utah's #1 fishery — 1.5M angling hours/year. Rainbow trout are sterile (triploid) to protect cutthroat genetics. Kokanee stocked annually. AFS Project of the Year 2006.

bestSeasons.iceFishing: December–March (very popular — thick reliable ice at 7,600 ft)

windPatterns.dominant: W/NW frontal winds. SE drainage from surrounding mountains. Exposed alpine setting amplifies wind.
windPatterns.hazards: High elevation = fast-changing weather. Afternoon gusts can reach 24-28 knots during active systems. Thunderstorms June–September.
windPatterns.thermalPattern: Easterly to SE in morning, shifting W/NW in afternoon. Frontal systems produce strongest sustained winds.
windPatterns.bestCalm: Early morning (6-9 AM), periods between frontal passages

nearbyStations.notes: UTCOP (US-40 Co-Op Creek) is ground truth — closest to reservoir. UTDAN (Daniels Summit) covers north pass. DSTU1 (Daniels-Strawberry SNOTEL) for snow/wind at 8,007 ft. No ASOS within 30 miles.

fishSpecies.notes: One of Utah's top walleye fisheries. Rainbow trout grow to 20+ inches. Brown trout provide trophy opportunities.

windPatterns.dominant: Duchesne River valley channeling. Afternoon thermals from surrounding basin.
windPatterns.hazards: Moderate — less exposed than Bear Lake but afternoon gusts can surprise

nearbyStations.notes: KVEL (Vernal Regional Airport) is ~30 miles east. Limited close-range stations. Duchesne area has RAWS stations.

primary: lake trout (trophy — UT record 51 lb 8 oz)
fishSpecies.notes: Trophy lake trout fishery — fish commonly exceed 30 lbs. Kokanee salmon provide excellent trolling. Burbot (freshwater ling cod) are an invasive target species.

windPatterns.dominant: Generally moderate — canyon topography provides some shelter. SE winds typical, averaging 5-12 mph.
windPatterns.hazards: Canyon funneling in narrow sections. Afternoon thunderstorms June–August. Exposed open-water sections near Lucerne/Buckboard can get significant chop.
windPatterns.bestCalm: Morning hours before 11 AM. High elevation keeps summer temperatures moderate (~80°F).

nearbyStations.notes: KFGR (Flaming Gorge/Dutch John) is nearby AWOS. Cart Creek RAWS (utCART) and Kings Point RAWS (utKING) are nearby fire weather stations in the recreation area.

fishSpecies.notes: Striped bass are the signature species — 5-30 lb fighters. Striper boils (surface feeding frenzies) are legendary. Low water levels have dramatically changed fishing patterns since 2020.

windPatterns.dominant: Canyon winds highly variable by location. Narrow canyons funnel and amplify winds. Monsoon season (July–September) brings violent afternoon thunderstorms.
windPatterns.hazards: HIGH HAZARD. Microbursts can develop in under a minute — 56 mph+ documented. Canyon funneling creates sudden strong gusts in slot canyons. Afternoon thunderstorms during monsoon season produce flash floods in canyons. Low water levels narrow channels and increase congestion.
windPatterns.thermalPattern: Calm mornings typically give way to afternoon canyon winds. Desert heating drives strong thermal circulation.
windPatterns.bestCalm: Early morning before 10 AM. Winter months are calmest overall.

nearbyStations.notes: KPGA (Page, AZ Airport) is the closest ASOS — located at the dam. Extremely limited weather coverage across the 186-mile lake. Bullfrog and Halls Crossing areas have minimal real-time wind data. Significant coverage gap for boaters.

fishSpecies.notes: Ogden Valley warmwater fishery. Excellent bass fishing. Tiger muskie provide trophy opportunities. Popular summer recreation lake.

windPatterns.dominant: E/W canyon channeling through Ogden Canyon. Afternoon thermals from Ogden Valley.
windPatterns.hazards: Canyon mouth (west end) can experience amplified winds. Generally moderate.

nearbyStations.notes: COOPOGNU1 (Pineview Dam) is ground truth. KOGD (Ogden Airport) is closest ASOS. OGP (Mt Ogden/Snowbasin) is high-elevation ridge indicator.

fishSpecies.notes: Small but productive family fishing reservoir. Regularly stocked with rainbow trout.

windPatterns.dominant: Cache Valley wind patterns — south drainage common
windPatterns.hazards: Minimal — small lake

nearbyStations.notes: KLGU (Logan-Cache Airport) is closest ASOS, approximately 10 miles north.

fishSpecies.notes: Diverse trout and warmwater fishery. Trophy brown trout and smallmouth bass. Kokanee provide summer trolling.

windPatterns.dominant: Canyon-channeled winds. East Canyon corridor funnels wind from both directions.
windPatterns.hazards: Moderate — narrow canyon can amplify gusts

nearbyStations.notes: No nearby ASOS — KSLC is 25+ miles west over the Wasatch. East Canyon area is a weather station coverage gap. UDOT road weather stations along I-80 corridor may provide some data.

fishSpecies.notes: Versatile warmwater and trout fishery. Convenient to Salt Lake City via I-80. Wiper provide exciting surface action.

windPatterns.dominant: Weber River valley channeling. I-80 corridor winds.
windPatterns.hazards: Moderate — open water sections can get afternoon chop

nearbyStations.notes: No dedicated ASOS nearby. I-80 UDOT road weather stations along the corridor provide wind data. Coalville area.

fishSpecies.notes: Beautiful Weber River reservoir in Summit County. Excellent shore and boat fishing. Rainbow trout are primary target.

windPatterns.dominant: Weber River corridor channeling. Afternoon thermals from surrounding hills.
windPatterns.hazards: Moderate — summit county elevation means faster weather changes

nearbyStations.notes: No dedicated ASOS. Located between Park City and Coalville. Limited real-time wind data.

fishSpecies.notes: One of Utah's largest warmwater fisheries — 22 miles long. Northern pike and tiger muskie provide apex predator fishing. Wiper are aggressive summer targets.

windPatterns.dominant: Sevier River valley winds. Exposed open terrain means wind can build across long fetch.
windPatterns.hazards: Long fetch (22 miles) allows significant wave buildup. Afternoon winds common.

nearbyStations.notes: KPVU is 50+ miles north. Nephi area may have RAWS or CoCoRaHS stations. Limited real-time coverage. Significant weather station gap.

fishSpecies.notes: Special regulations: artificial flies and lures only (no bait). Promotes trophy-sized trout. Known for quality over quantity.

windPatterns.dominant: Open high-desert exposure. Beaver River valley winds.
windPatterns.hazards: Afternoon winds common in exposed basin

nearbyStations.notes: KCDC (Cedar City Regional Airport) is ~35 miles south. Brimstone Reservoir RAWS station is in the general area. Limited close-range data.

fishSpecies.notes: Blue Ribbon fishery. Known as a trout 'factory' — stocked fish grow rapidly due to high productivity. ~220,000 sub-catchable rainbow trout stocked annually in fall.

windPatterns.dominant: East Fork Sevier River valley winds. Open plateau exposure.
windPatterns.hazards: Moderate — afternoon wind common but generally manageable

nearbyStations.notes: Otter Creek RAWS (utOTTE) is nearby fire weather station. KCDC (Cedar City) is distant. Limited real-time data.

fishSpecies.notes: Utah's largest natural mountain lake. Trophy mackinaw in deep water (60-100+ ft). Splake provide unique hybrid trout fishing. High elevation means reliable long ice season.

bestSeasons.lakeTrout: Year-round, best May–June (post ice-off) and October–November
bestSeasons.iceFishing: December–March (mackinaw jigging at depth, perch in shallows)

windPatterns.dominant: Mountain thermals from surrounding Fishlake National Forest. SW prevailing winds.
windPatterns.hazards: High elevation (8,848 ft) means rapid weather changes. Afternoon thunderstorms June–September.

nearbyStations.notes: Very remote — KCDC (Cedar City) is 70+ miles south. SNOTEL stations in Fishlake National Forest provide snow and temperature data. Major weather station coverage gap.

fishSpecies.notes: Treated with rotenone in 2006 to remove invasive Utah chub. Restocked — population recovered by 2016. Located on Markagunt Plateau near Cedar Breaks.

windPatterns.dominant: Mountain plateau winds. Generally moderate due to surrounding forest.
windPatterns.hazards: High elevation = rapid weather changes, afternoon thunderstorms in summer

nearbyStations.notes: KCDC (Cedar City) is closest ASOS, ~25 miles SW. Brian Head/Cedar Breaks area may have SNOTEL or RAWS stations.

fishSpecies.notes: Blue Ribbon fishery. Highest-elevation state park in Utah. Special regs: cutthroat/tiger trout 15-22 inches must be released. Combined trout limit of 4. Upper Fish Creek is one of Utah's best wild cutthroat streams.

windPatterns.dominant: Wasatch Plateau drainage. W/NW frontal winds similar to Strawberry.
windPatterns.hazards: High elevation — rapid weather changes. Exposed to plateau winds.

nearbyStations.notes: KPUC (Price/Carbon County Airport) is ~25 miles east. Scofield area has AIS checkpoint station. Same general weather regime as Strawberry Reservoir.

fishSpecies.notes: Popular Vernal-area reservoir with sandy beach. Good mix of trout and warmwater species.

windPatterns.dominant: Uinta Basin winds — generally moderate. Ashley Creek valley influence.
windPatterns.hazards: Moderate — afternoon thermals

nearbyStations.notes: KVEL (Vernal Regional Airport) is ~7 miles south. Little Mountain RAWS (utLITT) nearby.

fishSpecies.notes: Scenic red-rock reservoir near Vernal. Rainbow and brown trout are primary targets. Dinosaur trackway nearby.

windPatterns.dominant: Big Brush Creek valley influence. Moderate exposure.
windPatterns.hazards: Generally moderate — red rock terrain provides some shelter

nearbyStations.notes: KVEL (Vernal Regional Airport) is ~10 miles SW. Same station coverage as Steinaker.

fishSpecies.notes: Diverse multi-species fishery. Recent crappie, white bass, and smallmouth bass introductions expanding fishing opportunities.

windPatterns.dominant: Sevier River valley winds. Tushar Mountains to the west influence drainage patterns.
windPatterns.hazards: Moderate — open valley exposure

nearbyStations.notes: KCDC (Cedar City) is ~60 miles south. Junction, UT area. Very limited close-range weather data.

fishSpecies.notes: Small reservoir near Escalante. Trout 10-14 inches bite actively spring/fall. Bluegill provide summer panfishing around shore vegetation.

windPatterns.dominant: Escalante valley winds. Generally light due to small size and surrounding terrain.
windPatterns.hazards: Minimal — very small lake

nearbyStations.notes: KPGA (Page, AZ) is ~80 miles south. Extremely remote — virtually no real-time weather data. Escalante area RAWS may exist.

fishSpecies.notes: Premier alpine trophy trout fishery. Special regulations: artificial flies and lures only. Fish over 20 inches common. Located in Manti-La Sal National Forest at 9,300 ft.

windPatterns.dominant: Mountain exposure at 9,300 ft — strong plateau winds possible
windPatterns.hazards: High elevation = extremely rapid weather changes. Afternoon thunderstorms.

nearbyStations.notes: KPUC (Price) is ~30 miles east and 4,000 ft lower. Elevation difference means airport data poorly represents reservoir conditions. Skyline Drive area UDOT stations may be relevant.

fishSpecies.notes: Huntington Creek reservoir. Deep water at 8,579 ft. Lake trout provide trophy deep-water fishing. Kokanee salmon stocked.

windPatterns.dominant: Wasatch Plateau exposure. Similar to Scofield/Huntington regime.
windPatterns.hazards: High elevation — rapid weather changes

nearbyStations.notes: Same coverage as Scofield/Huntington area. KPUC (Price) is nearest ASOS but elevation difference is significant.

fishSpecies.notes: Trophy tiger muskie fishery — fish reach 40+ inches. Diverse trout assemblage. Beautiful high-country setting near Orangeville.

windPatterns.dominant: Manti-La Sal area drainage. Valley orientation channels winds.
windPatterns.hazards: Moderate — canyon setting provides some shelter

nearbyStations.notes: KPUC (Price/Carbon County) is ~30 miles east. Limited close-range data.

fishSpecies.notes: Mountain reservoir near Brigham City. Family-friendly fishing. Trout best in spring/fall, bluegill popular in summer.

windPatterns.dominant: Wellsville Mountain corridor. Box Elder County winds.
windPatterns.hazards: Generally light — sheltered mountain valley

nearbyStations.notes: KBMC (Brigham City Regional Airport) is closest ASOS, ~5 miles west.

fishSpecies.notes: Wakeless boating only — narrow, remote reservoir. Rainbow and cutthroat trout. Primitive facilities, no on-site camping.

windPatterns.dominant: Canyon-sheltered. Lost Creek drainage.
windPatterns.hazards: Minimal — narrow canyon, wakeless-only
windPatterns.bestCalm: Most of the day — sheltered setting

nearbyStations.notes: KOGD (Ogden) is ~25 miles west. Very remote — no nearby weather stations. Morgan County.

fishSpecies.notes: No-wake reservoir — ideal for kayaks, float tubes, canoes. Steep shorelines. Stocked trout 10-16 inches. Kokanee available outside fall spawning season.

windPatterns.dominant: South Fork Ogden River drainage. Sheltered canyon.
windPatterns.hazards: Minimal — small, sheltered, no-wake
windPatterns.bestCalm: Most of day due to canyon shelter

nearbyStations.notes: KOGD (Ogden) is nearest ASOS. Remote canyon location in Wasatch Mountains east of Ogden.

fishSpecies.notes: Warm-water Dixie reservoir near Hurricane. Year-round fishing possible due to low elevation. Winter trout stocking, summer bass.

windPatterns.dominant: Hurricane area desert thermals. Can be windy in exposed terrain.
windPatterns.hazards: Strong afternoon thermals in summer. Hot winds.

nearbyStations.notes: KSGU (St. George Municipal Airport) is ~15 miles south.

fishSpecies.notes: Utah's newest reservoir (2003). Outstanding warmwater bass fishery. 8-9 lb bass caught. Red sandstone setting.

windPatterns.dominant: Desert thermals. Similar to Quail Creek.
windPatterns.hazards: Afternoon winds in exposed desert terrain

nearbyStations.notes: KSGU (St. George) is closest ASOS.

fishSpecies.notes: Known for large but temperamental bass. 15-17 miles NW of St. George.

windPatterns.dominant: Santa Clara River valley winds. Moderate exposure.
windPatterns.hazards: Afternoon desert thermals

nearbyStations.notes: KSGU (St. George) is nearest ASOS.

fishSpecies.notes: Blue Ribbon warmwater fishery. Outstanding bluegill and largemouth bass. Shallow, weedy habitat. New AIS decontamination station installed.

windPatterns.dominant: Uinta Basin winds. Shallow lake = fast chop buildup.
windPatterns.hazards: Very shallow — similar wind-wave dynamics to Utah Lake on a smaller scale

nearbyStations.notes: KVEL (Vernal) is ~25 miles east.

primary: cutthroat trout (Colorado River & Bear River strains)
fishSpecies.notes: Blue Ribbon fishery near Zion National Park. Special regs: artificial flies and lures only at times. Accessed by unmaintained dirt roads April–December.

windPatterns.dominant: Mountain setting between Zion and Pine Valley. Generally moderate.
windPatterns.hazards: Minimal — mountain-sheltered

nearbyStations.notes: KCDC (Cedar City) is ~30 miles north. Remote location.

fishSpecies.notes: Quality rainbow trout (12-16 inches) and smallmouth bass in scenic red rock setting.

windPatterns.dominant: Iron County high-desert winds.
windPatterns.hazards: Moderate afternoon winds

nearbyStations.notes: KCDC (Cedar City) is ~25 miles NE.

ASOS_AWOS.description: FAA/NWS airport observation stations — most reliable, continuous data

RAWS.description: Remote Automated Weather Stations — fire weather focus, typically 10-min reporting

utOTTE.name: Otter Creek RAWS
utOTTE.notes: Closest fire weather station to Otter Creek and Piute reservoirs

UDOT_RWIS.description: Utah DOT Road Weather Information System — wind, temp, road surface data

UTALP.name: Point of the Mountain I-15
UTALP.notes: Key Utah Lake north flow indicator

SKY.name: Skyline Drive UDOT
SKY.notes: Wasatch Plateau crest — relevant to all eastern slope reservoirs

SNOTEL.description: NRCS Snow Telemetry — high-elevation snow, temp, some wind data

MESONET_OTHER.description: MesoWest mesonet, industrial, CoCoRaHS, ski area stations

FPS.name: Flight Park South
FPS.notes: Paragliding wind station — excellent thermal indicator

QSF.name: Spanish Fork
QSF.notes: SE thermal early warning for Utah Lake

BERU1.name: Bear River
BERU1.notes: DCP station near Bear Lake area

COVERAGE_GAPS.description: Lakes with poor or no real-time weather station coverage — priority areas for future station installation or WeatherFlow/Davis integration

critical.issue: 186-mile reservoir with only KPGA at the dam. Bullfrog, Halls Crossing, and upper lake have zero real-time wind data.
critical.recommendation: NPS stations, WeatherFlow buoy stations, or Synoptic partner installations at Bullfrog/Halls Crossing/Hite

critical.issue: KLGU is 45+ miles away. BERU1 is nearest but not on the lake. No real-time lake-surface wind data.
critical.recommendation: WeatherFlow station at Cisco Beach or Rendezvous Beach marina

critical.issue: KCDC is 70+ miles south. No close-range stations at 8,848 ft.
critical.recommendation: SNOTEL or RAWS station at Fish Lake lodge area

critical.issue: 22-mile reservoir with no nearby ASOS. Nearest is 50+ miles.
critical.recommendation: RAWS or mesonet station near Yuba State Park

moderate.issue: Junction, UT area — KCDC 60 miles away
Escalante is very remote — KPGA 80 miles
Mountain canyon setting — no nearby ASOS
I-80 corridor but no dedicated ASOS
9,300 ft — KPUC is 4,000 ft lower, poor representation
